info.title: ReasonDB API
info.description:
A reasoning-native database API for AI agents. ReasonDB uses LLM-guided tree traversal for intelligent document search, optimized for AI agent workflows.

## Key Features

- **Hierarchical Document Storage**: Documents stored as navigable trees, not flat chunks
- **LLM-Guided Retrieval**: AI reasons through tree structure using summaries
- **Parallel Branch Exploration**: Concurrent traversal with beam search
- **Multi-Format Support**: PDFs, Markdown, HTML, and more
- **RQL Query Language**: SQL-like queries with SEARCH and REASON clauses

tags.name: tables
tags.description: Table (collection) management - organize documents into logical groups

tags.name: ingestion
tags.description: Document ingestion - import text, files, and URLs into hierarchical trees

tags.name: search
tags.description: LLM-guided search - intelligent tree traversal with reasoning

tags.name: query
tags.description: RQL queries - SQL-like queries with SEARCH and REASON clauses

tags.name: documents
tags.description: Document management - list, view, and delete documents

get.description: Returns OK if the server is running.

post.description: Creates a new table (collection) to organize documents. Documents can be assigned to tables during ingestion or moved later.

get.description: Returns a list of all tables in the database, including document counts.
Returns detailed information about a specific table.

patch.description: Update table metadata, name, or description. Only provided fields are updated.

delete.description: Delete a table. By default, documents in the table are moved to the default table. Set `cascade=true` in the request body to delete all documents in the table.

get.description: Returns all documents assigned to a specific table.

post.description: Performs an intelligent search across documents using an LLM to navigate the hierarchical tree structure. The LLM evaluates each node's summary to decide which branches to explore, mimicking human reasoning. Returns relevant content with extracted answers and confidence scores.

Execute SQL-like queries against documents using ReasonDB Query Language (RQL).

Supports:
- `WHERE` clauses for filtering by tags, author, metadata
- `SEARCH` clause for BM25 full-text search (fast keyword matching)
- `REASON` clause for LLM semantic search (intelligent answer extraction)
- `GROUP BY` and aggregate functions (COUNT, SUM, AVG)
- `EXPLAIN` to view query execution plans

get.description: Returns a summary of all ingested documents in the database.

patch.description: Partially updates a document's title, table assignment, tags, or metadata. Only provided fields are changed. `metadata` is merged key-by-key, not replaced wholesale.

get.description: Returns full details for a specific document including metadata and tree info.

delete.description: Deletes a document and all its associated nodes (cascade delete).

get.summary: Get all nodes for a document
get.description: Returns a flat list of all nodes in the document tree.

patch.description: Partially updates a single node by its document and node IDs. Only provided fields are changed. `metadata.attributes` is merged key-by-key. `cross_ref_node_ids` is replaced when provided.

get.description: Returns the complete hierarchical tree structure with nested children. Useful for visualizing or navigating the document structure.

post.description:
Enqueue plain text or Markdown content for background ingestion. The content will be chunked, organized into a tree structure, and optionally summarized by the LLM.

Returns a `job_id`. Poll `GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}` for status and the resulting document ID.

Upload a document file for ingestion. Supports PDF, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, images (with OCR), audio files (with transcription), HTML, and more via MarkItDown.

The multipart body only needs the `file` field.

Enqueue content from a URL for background ingestion. Supports web pages, YouTube videos (with transcription), and other online resources.

Returns a `job_id`. Poll `GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}` for status and the resulting document ID.

Enqueue multiple text documents for background ingestion in a single request. Each item is processed as an individual document.

Returns a `job_id`. Poll `GET /v1/jobs/{job_id}` for status.

metadata.description: Metadata to merge (key-by-key, not replaced wholesale)

domain_vocab.description: Domain-specific vocabulary and terminology used by the LLM reasoning engine to interpret documents in this table.

context.description: Contextual description for LLM reasoning. Provides background about what this table contains and how documents should be interpreted.
context.example: This table contains legal contracts and agreements for US jurisdictions.

instructions.description: Custom directives that guide how the LLM should answer questions or traverse documents within this table.
instructions.example: Always cite clause numbers when referencing contract terms.

tags.description: Classification tags for grouping and discovery of this table.

cascade.description: If true, delete all documents in the table. If false, move them to default table.

context.example: This table contains legal contracts for US jurisdictions.

instructions.description: Custom LLM query and reasoning directives
instructions.example: Always cite clause numbers when referencing contract terms.

document_count.description: Number of documents in this table

total_nodes.description: Total number of nodes in the tree

query.description: The natural language query to search for
query.example: What are the key benefits of machine learning?

document_id.description: Optional document ID to search within (searches all if not provided)

table_id.description: Optional table ID to restrict search to

metadata.description: Filter by document metadata (author, contract_type, etc.)

node_id.description: Node ID where content was found

path.description: Path from root to this node (breadcrumbs)

content.description: The relevant content at this node
content.example: Machine learning enables computers to learn from data...

answer.example: The key benefits include automation, pattern recognition, and predictive capabilities.

reasoning.description: LLM's reasoning for selecting this path
reasoning.example: This chapter covers ML fundamentals relevant to the query

nodes_pruned.description: Nodes pruned (not explored due to low confidence)

llm_calls.description: Number of LLM API calls made

query.example: SELECT * FROM legal WHERE author = 'Alice' LIMIT 10

score.description: Relevance score (BM25 for SEARCH, confidence for REASON)

confidence.description: Confidence score from LLM (for REASON queries)

step_type.description: Step type (e.g., TableScan, IndexScan, Filter, Aggregate)

description.description: Description of what this step does

root_node_id.description: Root node ID of the tree

summary.example: This chapter covers the fundamentals of neural networks...

is_leaf.description: Whether this is a leaf node (no children)

summary.example: This chapter covers neural network fundamentals...

generate_summaries.description: Whether to generate LLM summaries (default: true)

url.description: URL to ingest (web page, YouTube video, etc.)

generate_summaries.description: Whether to generate LLM summaries (default: true)
Whether to generate LLM summaries (default: true)

progress.description: Progress message (only present when status is 'processing')

result.description: Ingestion result (only present when status is 'completed')

error.description: Error message (only present when status is 'failed')

UpdateDocumentRequest.description: Request body for updating a document. All fields are optional; only provided fields are changed.

table_id.description: Move document to a different table

metadata.description: Metadata to merge (key-by-key, not replaced wholesale)

UpdateNodeMetadataRequest.description: Partial update for node metadata fields. All fields are optional.

page_number.description: Page number in the source document

attributes.description: Custom key-value attributes to merge into the node (string values only)

cross_ref_node_ids.description: IDs of sibling nodes this node explicitly cross-references (replaces existing list)

UpdateNodeRequest.description: Request body for updating a document node. All fields are optional; only provided fields are changed.

summary.description: Updated LLM summary used during tree traversal
summary.example: This chapter covers neural network fundamentals including backpropagation.

content.description: Updated leaf content (only meaningful for leaf nodes)
content.example: Neural networks are computing systems loosely inspired by biological neural networks...

image_path.description: Updated path to associated image for vision-enabled reasoning

NodeDetail.description: Full details for a single document node.

summary.description: LLM-generated summary used for tree traversal
summary.example: This chapter covers neural network fundamentals...

is_leaf.description: Whether this is a leaf node (no children)

content.description: Actual content (only present for leaf nodes)

image_path.description: Path to associated image for vision-enabled reasoning

parent_id.description: Parent node ID (null for root nodes)

page_number.description: Page number in the source document

cross_ref_node_ids.description: IDs of sibling nodes this node cross-references

ApiKeyAuth.name: X-Api-Key
ApiKeyAuth.description: API key for authentication. Only required when the server is started with `--auth-enabled`. Obtain a key via `POST /v1/auth/keys`.
